source_snippet:
This is the FAaaS workstream to address critical security vulnerabilities from Mythos scan, resolve issues and assist in fleet rollout activities.

* FAaaS PMO ticket: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/browse/FAAASPMO-1263>
* Internal FAaaS slack chanel: [#internal-faaas-scan-kiwi](https://oracle.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C08PB5J87C5)
* External FAaaS slack channel with SaaS Security POCs: [#apps-division-scan-kiwi](https://oracle.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0AUEDVPU8K)

  


FAaaS Kiwi Workstreams
----------------------

### #1 Security scanning and fixing critical findings

1. Current scan status: 89% complete (237 of 266 scanned, 29 remaining) -  [SaaS dashboard link](https://saasinsights.oraclecorp.com/ords/r/saaskpi/a1004/kiwi-saas-security-dashboard)
2. FAaaS Repos to scan: 266 FAaaS repos to provided SaaS security in new format: 
	1. Sampath has provided 28 additional FAaaS RM repos for FAAASSRE JIRA - [devops-coderepository link](https://devops.oci.oraclecorp.com/devops-coderepository/projects/ocid1.devopsproject.oc1.phx.amaaaaaaw4vcxbyalo5gavleayvmmkytburb5xxytnb5ysd3ltvnwdryqztq/repositories?_ctx=us-phoenix-1%2Cdevops_scm_central)
3. FAaaS Jiras:
	1. FAaaS Kiwi JIRA dashboard: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/secure/Dashboard.jspa?selectPageId=73429>
	2. FAaaS Kiwi JIRA filter: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/issues/?filter=219921>
4. Working group with engineers to accelerate the issue triage and resolution process. Sunil has scheduled on Monday 5/18
	1. ADP: Sang, Norman
	2. FACP: Matthias
	3. HCP: Arup, Abhay, Fang

### #2 Fleet Patching

1. Leadership ask is to come up with a proposal for monthly full fleet patching in June and beyond. Options are under review (**Siju**, **Venkatesh**) - [Patching proposal image](https://confluence.oraclecorp.com/confluence/download/attachments/20352556426/image-2026-5-7_18-0-13.png?version=1&modificationDate=1778191217000&api=v2)
2. Patching proposal reviewed on 5/12 Strategic Ops -
3. From Preeti, for the June tactical + future support for fleetwide patching, there are some ERs we need filed / tracked as part of the program as far as Refresh goes:
	1. We need to support bulk cancel API to get pre-existing conflicting schedules out of the way.
	2. We need to get a finalized blurb for customer notification assuming we want to notify the customer why their Refresh was canceled.

### #3 DB Patching

1. One round of Ksplice patching done:
	1. PROD Clusters: Total: 854/855 Completed, Pending: 1 (eiaa1a030 - Inactive cluster . Nothing to patch)
	2. Non-PROD Clusters (Pre-Prod, PINT, Beta): All Completed (209/209)
	3. DEMO Clusters: All Completed (93/93)
2. For non-Ksplice patching, Currently only 2 slots of 3 days each are available per month. In order to do full fleet, Sravan needs 5 slots of 3 days each
3. We need clarity from leadership if DB patching every month is a requirement and exactly what types of patching (DomU, Dom0, GI)

### #4 OS Patching

1. Ed is working with Erna's team on OS patching of Linux image

Appendix - Supporting Materials
-------------------------------

FAaaS repo list: <https://confluence.oraclecorp.com/confluence/display/OCIFACP/FAaaS+Repos+for+Security+Scanning>

SaaS Kiwi dashboard: [https://saasinsights.oraclecorp.com/ords/r/saaskpi/a1004/kiwi-saas-security-dashboard](https://oracle-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/mauruthi_geetha_mohan_oracle_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BC3BB40F4-2031-48A1-B6E0-353419DCCEB7%7D&file=kiwi-priority.xlsx&wdLOR=c1E39D1D1-9F92-0444-A12F-1DA0E11A44AA&nav=MTVfe0IwQzdFNERGLUYzNjUtNEM3Ri1BQTA3LTVCMDREMTgxNkI4Q30&action=default&mobileredirect=true)

SaaS Kiwi xls: [kiwi-priority.xlsx](https://oracle-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/mauruthi_geetha_mohan_oracle_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BC3BB40F4-2031-48A1-B6E0-353419DCCEB7%7D&file=kiwi-priority.xlsx&wdLOR=c1E39D1D1-9F92-0444-A12F-1DA0E11A44AA&nav=MTVfe0IwQzdFNERGLUYzNjUtNEM3Ri1BQTA3LTVCMDREMTgxNkI4Q30&action=default&mobileredirect=true)

expected_extraction.program_purpose: Address critical security vulnerabilities from Mythos scan, resolve issues, and assist in fleet rollout activities.
expected_extraction.rag_summary: Amber — Security scanning is 89% complete (237/266; 29 remaining) and DB Ksplice patching is essentially complete, but fleet/DB non-Ksplice patching are constrained by limited monthly slots and pending leadership decisions on monthly patching requirements and full-fleet approach.
expected_extraction.schedule_health: Schedule is At risk because monthly full fleet patching options for June+ are still under review and DB non-Ksplice patching capacity is short (2 slots/month available vs 5 needed); mitigation is to secure leadership decisions on patching cadence/scope and file/track required ERs to unblock fleetwide execution.

key_metrics.metric: DB Ksplice patching - PROD clusters completed/total
key_metrics.details: Pending: 1 (eiaa1a030 - inactive cluster; nothing to patch)

key_metrics.metric: DB Ksplice patching - Non-PROD clusters completed/total
DB Ksplice patching - DEMO clusters completed/total
DB non-Ksplice patching capacity (needed for full fleet)

blocking_issues.description: Leadership decision needed on proposal for monthly full fleet patching in June and beyond (options under review).
ER dependency: support bulk cancel API to remove pre-existing conflicting schedules for Refresh fleetwide patching support.
ER dependency: finalize customer notification blurb for why Refresh was canceled (if customer notification is desired).
Capacity gap for DB non-Ksplice patching: only 2 monthly 3-day slots available vs 5 required for full fleet.
Leadership clarity needed on whether monthly DB patching is required and what patch types are in scope (DomU, Dom0, GI).

next_steps.action: Hold the engineer working group to accelerate issue triage and resolution.
Review/align on monthly full fleet patching proposal for June and beyond following Strategic Ops review.
File and begin tracking ERs required for June tactical + future fleetwide patching support (bulk cancel API; customer notification blurb).
Obtain leadership clarity on whether DB patching every month is required and confirm patching scope (DomU, Dom0, GI).

exec_asks.ask: Approve/decide on the proposal for monthly full fleet patching in June and beyond.
exec_asks.consequence_if_delayed: June+ fleetwide patching approach remains under review, delaying execution planning and rollout.

exec_asks.ask: Decide whether DB patching every month is a requirement and confirm which patch types are required (DomU, Dom0, GI).
exec_asks.consequence_if_delayed: DB non-Ksplice planning cannot be finalized and capacity constraints (2 slots/month vs 5 needed) will continue to block full-fleet execution.

workstream_status_snapshot.workstream_name: #1 Security scanning and fixing critical findings
workstream_status_snapshot.current_status: Scanning is 89% complete (237/266) with 29 repos remaining, and a working group is scheduled to accelerate triage and resolution.
workstream_status_snapshot.primary_risk: Remaining repos and critical finding remediation may lag without sustained engineering triage capacity.

workstream_status_snapshot.current_status: Monthly full fleet patching proposal for June+ is under review and was reviewed in Strategic Ops on 5/12; ERs are identified for Refresh support.
workstream_status_snapshot.primary_risk: Execution is gated on leadership decision/approval and on filing/tracking required ERs (bulk cancel API, customer notification blurb).

workstream_status_snapshot.current_status: Ksplice patching round is effectively complete across PROD (854/855), Non-PROD (209/209), and DEMO (93/93); non-Ksplice full-fleet patching is constrained by limited monthly slots.
workstream_status_snapshot.primary_risk: Capacity shortfall (2 slots/month available vs 5 needed) and unclear leadership requirements on monthly cadence/scope.

workstream_status_snapshot.current_status: OS patching of the Linux image is in progress with Ed working with Erna's team.
workstream_status_snapshot.primary_risk: Progress and timeline are not stated, creating visibility risk on completion timing.

leadership_decisions_needed.decision: Select/approve the approach for monthly full fleet patching in June and beyond.
Confirm whether DB patching every month is required and define patching scope (DomU, Dom0, GI).

dependencies_and_ers.description: ER needed to support bulk cancel API to clear pre-existing conflicting schedules for Refresh.
ER needed to finalize customer notification blurb explaining why Refresh was canceled (if notifying customers).
